              - `packages/db/prisma/schema.prisma`: +AlertHistory model (id, tenantId, alertRuleId?, eventId?, firedAt, matchedPriority, recipientCount, ruleNameSnapshot, eventTitleSnapshot + 4 indexes including composite [tenantId, firedAt]); +backlinks `alertHistory AlertHistory[]` on Tenant/Event/AlertRule.
              - `packages/db/prisma/migrations/20260512044045_add_alert_history/`: Prisma-generated migration.sql (CREATE TABLE alert_history + 4 indexes + 3 FK constraints — tenant RESTRICT, alertRule SET NULL, event SET NULL) + down.sql (reversible: drops constraints/indexes/table in safe order).
              - `packages/jobs/src/processors/alerts.processor.ts`: added `alertHistory` to typedTx shape; one `typedTx.alertHistory.create(...)` call per matching rule INSIDE the existing $transaction, AFTER the recipient loop — captures matchedPriority, recipientCount, ruleNameSnapshot, eventTitleSnapshot so the audit trail survives later rule/event deletion.
              - `packages/jobs/src/__tests__/alerts.processor.test.ts`: +`alertHistory: { create: vi.fn() }` in mockTx via vi.hoisted; +2 new test cases — (f) one history row per matching rule with snapshot fields populated (NOT per recipient — verified with 2-recipient fixture asserting notification.create×2 and alertHistory.create×1), (g) multiple matching rules write multiple history rows. RED→GREEN order confirmed (initial run showed 2 failed / 5 passed; post-implementation 7/7 pass).
              - `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/alertHistory.ts`: new router with `list` endpoint — cursor pagination, optional alertRuleId filter, tenant-scoped via tenantProcedure, includes nested alertRule + event for display.
              - `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/__tests__/alertHistory.test.ts`: 5 router tests — tenant scoping, alertRuleId filter, pagination with nextCursor, no-cursor when within limit, UNAUTHORIZED when tenant missing.
              - `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/index.ts`: registered alertHistoryRouter.
              - `apps/web/src/app/(dashboard)/alerts/history/page.tsx`: new client page — table view (Fired At / Rule / Event / Priority badge / Recipients column) with cursor-based prev/next pagination; italic "(deleted)" indicator when alertRule or event was later deleted using the snapshot fields; Event column links to /events/[id] when event still exists.
              - `apps/web/src/app/(dashboard)/alerts/page.tsx`: added "View History" outlined Button as Link to /alerts/history in the header beside the "New Rule" Dialog trigger.
              - `apps/web/src/components/ui/table.tsx`: shadcn Table primitive installed via `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add table` (first use of Table in this repo).
              Migration applied to dev DB. Typecheck 6/6 clean, lint 5/5 clean, tests 57/57 pass (was 50/50 — added 2 processor tests + 5 router tests). 4 migrations total in repo now.

              EXECUTION NOTES:
              Per memory-governance §2.5b and the documented hook-injection thrashing lesson from earlier this session, Sonnet was bypassed up-front for this task. The scope assessment showed ~10 code files + 4 governance writes (score 49 on Tier 3 formula, just over the 40 threshold), but every Read call inside a subagent triggers vercel-plugin auto-suggesters (~1.5K per Read on `prisma/schema.prisma` and `apps/web/**` patterns) plus claude-mem prior-observation pointers (~500 per Read), so even tightly-scoped Tier-2 tasks blow Sonnet's 30K budget in practice. Opus-direct executed cleanly within ~50K total session tokens — well within the 100K ceiling and well under the 200K context window. No retries, no thrashing signals. Hook injection is still firing (~10× this session) but Opus 4.7 absorbs it without quality degradation.
